formulas.name: Basic Trigonometric Ratios
formulas.formula:
sin ? = Opposite/Hypotenuse
cos ? = Adjacent/Hypotenuse
tan ? = Opposite/Adjacent
cot ? = Adjacent/Opponent
sec ? = Hypotenuse/Adjacent
cosec ? = Hypotenuse/Opposite

examples.q: In right triangle, opposite = 3, adjacent = 4, hypotenuse = 5. Find sin 30°.

formulas.name: Fundamental Identities
formulas.formula:
sin²? + cos²? = 1
1 + tan²? = sec²?
1 + cot²? = cosec²?
tan ? = sin ?/cos ?

formulas.name: Complementary Angles
formulas.formula:
sin(90° - ?) = cos ?
cos(90° - ?) = sin ?
tan(90° - ?) = cot ?
cot(90° - ?) = tan ?

formulas.name: Sum and Difference Formulas
formulas.formula:
sin(A ± B) = sin A cos B ± cos A sin B
cos(A ± B) = cos A cos B ? sin A sin B
tan(A ± B) = (tan A ± tan B)/(1 ? tan A tan B)

formulas.name: Double Angle Formulas
formulas.formula:
sin 2? = 2 sin ? cos ?
cos 2? = cos²? - sin²? = 2cos²? - 1 = 1 - 2sin²?
tan 2? = 2 tan ?/(1 - tan²?)

formulas.name: Triple Angle Formulas
formulas.formula:
sin 3? = 3 sin ? - 4 sin³?
cos 3? = 4 cos³? - 3 cos ?
tan 3? = (3 tan ? - tan³?)/(1 - 3 tan²?)

examples.q: Find sin 60° using triple angle formula if sin 20° = 1/3.
Find sin 15° using half angle formula.

formulas.name: Product to Sum Formulas
formulas.formula:
sin A cos B = [sin(A+B) + sin(A-B)]/2
cos A cos B = [cos(A+B) + cos(A-B)]/2
sin A sin B = [cos(A-B) - cos(A+B)]/2

examples.q: Convert sin 30° cos 45° to sum form.

formulas.name: Sum to Product Formulas
formulas.formula:
sin A + sin B = 2 sin[(A+B)/2] cos[(A-B)/2]
sin A - sin B = 2 cos[(A+B)/2] sin[(A-B)/2]
cos A + cos B = 2 cos[(A+B)/2] cos[(A-B)/2]

examples.q: Convert sin 60° + sin 30° to product form.

formulas.name: Maximum and Minimum Values
formulas.formula:
sin ?, cos ?: Range [-1, 1]
tan ?, cot ?: Range (-?, ?)
sec ?, cosec ?: Range (-?, -1] U [1, ?)

examples.q: Find maximum value of sin ? + cos ?.

formulas.name: Height and Distance - Basic
formulas.formula:
Height = Distance × tan angle
Distance = Height/tan angle
Angle of elevation from ground to top

examples.q: Building height 50m, observer distance 50?3 m. Find angle of elevation.

formulas.name: Height and Distance - Advanced
formulas.formula:
Height of tower from two points:
h = d × tan ?1 × tan ?2/(tan ?2 - tan ?1)
Where d is distance between observation points

vars.d: Angles of elevation from two points

examples.q: From points 100m apart, angles of elevation are 30° and 45°. Find tower height.

formulas.name: Trigonometric Equations - General
formulas.formula:
sin ? = k ? ? = sin?¹(k) + 360°n or ? = 180° - sin?¹(k) + 360°n
cos ? = k ? ? = ±cos?¹(k) + 360°n
tan ? = k ? ? = tan?¹(k) + 180°n

vars.d: Constant value (-1 ? k ? 1 for sin and cos)

formulas.name: Inverse Trigonometric Functions
formulas.formula:
sin?¹(sin ?) = ?
cos?¹(cos ?) = ?
tan?¹(tan ?) = ?
Domain restrictions apply

formulas.name: Law of Sines
formulas.formula:
a/sin A = b/sin B = c/sin C = 2R
Where R is circumradius of triangle

examples.q: Triangle with A=30°, B=60°, a=4. Find b.

formulas.name: Law of Cosines
formulas.formula:
a² = b² + c² - 2bc cos A
b² = a² + c² - 2ac cos B
c² = a² + b² - 2ab cos C

examples.q: Triangle with sides a=5, b=7, c=8. Find angle A.
In triangle, a=7, b=5, A=60°. Find B using law of tangents.

formulas.name: Area of Triangle Using Trigonometry
formulas.formula:
Area = (1/2)ab sin C = (1/2)bc sin A = (1/2)ca sin B
Area = abc/(4R) where R is circumradius

examples.q: Triangle with sides a=5, b=8, included angle C=30°. Find area.

formulas.name: Radius of Circumcircle and Incircle
formulas.formula:
Circumradius R = abc/(4 × Area)
Inradius r = Area/s where s = (a+b+c)/2

examples.q: Express sin 60° in terms of half angle.

formulas.name: De Moivre's Theorem
formulas.formula: (cos ? + i sin ?)^n = cos(n?) + i sin(n?)

formulas.name: Trigonometric Series
formulas.formula:
Sum of sin series: sin ? + sin 2? + ... + sin n?
Sum of cos series: cos ? + cos 2? + ... + cos n?

formulas.name: Trigonometric Inequalities
formulas.formula:
For 0° < ? < 90°:
sin ? < ? < tan ?
cos ? < 1 < sec ?

examples.q: Arrange sin 30°, 30°, tan 30° in increasing order.

formulas.name: Trigonometric Identities - Multiple Angles
formulas.formula:
sin 3? = 4 sin ? cos²? - sin ?
cos 3? = 4 cos³? - 3 cos ?
sin 4? = 2 sin 2? cos 2?
cos 4? = cos²2? - sin²2?

examples.q: Simplify sin 4? in terms of sin ? and cos ?.
steps: sin 4? = 2 sin 2? cos 2? = 2(2 sin ? cos ?)(cos²? - sin²?)

formulas.name: Trigonometric Ratios in Different Quadrants
formulas.formula:
Quadrant I: All positive
Quadrant II: sin, cosec positive
Quadrant III: tan, cot positive
Quadrant IV: cos, sec positive

steps: 150° is in Quadrant II where cos is negative

formulas.name: Periodicity of Trigonometric Functions
formulas.formula:
sin ?, cos ?, sec ?, cosec ?: Period 360°
tan ?, cot ?: Period 180°

formulas.name: Graph of Trigonometric Functions
formulas.formula:
Amplitude = Maximum value - Minimum value)/2
Period = Distance for one complete cycle
Phase shift = Horizontal shift

formulas.name: Trigonometric Transformations
formulas.formula:
y = A sin(B? + C) + D
Amplitude = |A|, Period = 360°/|B|
Phase shift = -C/B, Vertical shift = D

examples.q: Find amplitude and period of y = 3 sin(2?).

formulas.name: Trigonometric Applications in Physics
formulas.formula:
Projectile motion:
Horizontal range = (v² sin 2?)/g
Maximum height = (v² sin²?)/(2g)
Time of flight = (2v sin ?)/g

examples.q: Projectile at 30° with v=20m/s. Find range (g=10m/s²).

formulas.name: Trigonometric Approximations
formulas.formula:
For small angles (in radians):
sin ? ? ?
cos ? ? 1 - ?²/2
tan ? ? ?

formulas.name: Trigonometric Identities - Advanced
formulas.formula:
sin A + sin B + sin C - sin(A+B+C) = 4 sin(A+B)/2 sin(B+C)/2 sin(C+A)/2
cos A + cos B + cos C + cos(A+B+C) = 4 cos(A+B)/2 cos(B+C)/2 cos(C+A)/2

examples.q: If A + B + C = 180°, find sin A + sin B + sin C.
steps: sin A + sin B + sin C - sin 180° = 4 sin(A+B)/2 sin(B+C)/2 sin(C+A)/2
sin A + sin B + sin C = 4 sin(90°-C/2) sin(90°-A/2) sin(90°-B/2)
